title: passiflora foetida var. orinocensis
intro: Passiflora foetida var. orinocensis is a variety of the Passiflora foetida plant, commonly known as the cow's foot or wild passionflower, which is a tropical vine native to the Americas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The plant is also valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and providing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Passiflora foetida var. orinocensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
